episodes.title: Dead Reckoning Ep. 5 | Kicked Out of Scientology Rehab With $8 — Now He's Getting Married
episodes.description:
Most people spend their whole life being the smartest person in the room — 
and die without ever really living. This episode is about the gap between 
who you are and who you were meant to become, and what it actually takes 
to close it.

Our first-ever guest, Tom, is 4 years sober and about to get married. 
What got him there? A Scientology rehab in Michigan, a minor seizure, 
getting kicked out with $25 and no way home, a Greyhound from Kalamazoo 
to Port Authority, a woman who bought him a carton of cigarettes and said 
"this is all you're getting" — and somewhere in all of that, a complete 
transformation.

We also get into:
→ The one part of Scientology rehab that actually works (and why any 
   program in the country should use it)
→ Why overthinking is what takes smart people out of recovery
→ Learning to have fun again without substances — and why if you skip 
   this step, sobriety won't last
→ The deathbed question: what would you be most relieved you did?
→ Perennial philosophy, string theory, and why all spiritual traditions 
   are looking at the same thing from different angles
→ How Silicon Valley CEOs push dopamine addiction to billions — while 
   personally going on 40-day technology detoxes
→ The beginner's mind: why the moment you think you've mastered 
   something is the moment you're in danger

Dead Reckoning is no GPS, no map — just the knowledge of where you 
started, how far you've come, and the direction you're moving in. 
Hosted by Saad and John. This is for the people still figuring it out.
──────────────────────────────
